justifications.completeness: Missed both pedestrians and three of four annotated vehicles, producing only a single vehicle entry.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some vehicle attributes (motion, trunk, doors) and a spatial tag but omits all pedestrian attributes and most vehicle instances, so richness is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes one spatial relation in the middle of the road but fails to capture pedestrian spatial context such as walking along the side and may misrepresent the scene by reporting only one relation.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and partially incorrect: it omits pedestrians, misses most vehicles, and provides limited and potentially inaccurate spatial information.

justifications.completeness: 'Detection lists a single truck and no pedestrians despite five vehicles and two people in ground truth, so highly incomplete.'
justifications.semantic_richness: 'Provides type, motion and door/trunk states and a spatial tag for one vehicle but omits most objects and attributes from the scene.'
justifications.spatial_relations: 'Gives a specific relation for the truck ('in the middle of the road') but fails to report relations for pedestrians and other vehicles, so partially correct.'
justifications.overall: 'Captures one plausible vehicle attribute but misses most entities and relations, resulting in low overall utility.'

justifications.completeness: Detected only one vehicle and no pedestrians while ground truth lists five vehicles and four pedestrians, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracts basic attributes for the truck (type, motion, doors, trunk, and a spatial relation) but omits almost all actors and richer person attributes and actions.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides one correct left-side parking relation for the truck but fails to describe spatial relations for the other four vehicles and four pedestrians.
justifications.overall: System correctly labels a single truck with some attributes but misses the majority of objects and relations, yielding low overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected one vehicle with attributes and an empty second entry but missed all three pedestrians and most vehicles from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes for a single vehicle (type, motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial_relations) but omits identities, many vehicles, and all pedestrian semantics.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague spatial relation for the truck is given while pedestrian spatial contexts and positions of other vehicles are absent.
justifications.overall: Captures one vehicle attribute correctly but is largely incomplete and misses pedestrians and most vehicle instances, limiting its overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one vehicle and omits all three pedestrians and other vehicles from the ground truth, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some useful attributes (vehicle type, motion status, trunk/doors, a spatial tag, and a brief scene description) are provided for a single object, but most object-level semantics and pedestrian actions are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only one coarse spatial relation is given and positional information for pedestrians and other vehicles is absent, so spatial coverage is poor.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains a small amount of accurate vehicle information but fails to detect multiple vehicles and all pedestrians, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only a single vehicle and no pedestrians, missing most annotated objects (4 pedestrians and 6 vehicles).
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes for the single detected vehicle (type, motion status, trunk and doors, spatial relation) but omits pedestrian attributes and additional vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a plausible left-side relation for the truck but fails to capture any relations for multiple pedestrians and other vehicles present in the scene.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse: it identifies one vehicle with some attributes but misses the majority of objects and semantic content in the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only one vehicle and no pedestrians despite six annotated persons and multiple vehicles in the ground truth, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes are provided for the single detected truck and a brief scene description is given, but detailed attributes for pedestrians, multiple vehicles, and interactions are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: The model correctly states the truck is on the left side of the road, but it provides no spatial relations for any pedestrians or the other vehicles present.
justifications.overall: The output accurately describes one vehicle and its left-side location but omits most objects and relations from the scene, yielding poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 pedestrian and no vehicles while ground truth contains 7 persons and 5 vehicles, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided a few attributes for a single person but missed many action/context labels (e.g., multiple walkers, a jaywalker, carrying/talking) and mislabelled 'crossing' versus 'walking'.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only reports 'on the road' for one person and a generic scene location, missing side-of-road distinctions and other pedestrians' spatial contexts.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and partly incorrect, missing most actors and many semantic details; overall quality is low.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian is reported and no vehicles are detected while the ground truth contains seven pedestrians and four vehicles, so the extraction is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides a few labels (atomic_action, simple_context, age, track_hint) for a single subject but omits most agents and many attributes such as transporting for one person and all vehicle properties.
justifications.spatial_relations: It supplies basic spatial cues ('in foreground', 'on road') for one subject which partially match some ground-truth contexts but misses side-of-road and jaywalking distinctions and spatial relations for other agents.
justifications.overall: Major omissions of multiple pedestrians and all vehicles combined with an incorrect atomic action/context for the detected subject result in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian and one partially described vehicle while ground truth contains five pedestrians and four vehicles, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels for a single pedestrian and one vehicle but omits many attributes present in ground truth such as communicative states, transporting details, and multiple object tracks.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a single coarse relation for the pedestrian and empty relations for vehicles, lacking detailed and consistent spatial descriptors.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and incomplete, with limited semantics and spatial information compared to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 10 annotated dynamic objects (7 pedestrians + 3 vehicles), so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected pedestrian the model provided multiple attributes (action, context, age) matching ground truth, but it failed to provide attributes for the majority of actors and missed vehicle attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations field is empty and the extraction omits relations between actors and vehicles despite contextual cues.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures one correct pedestrian with reasonable attribute detail but misses most actors, vehicles, and spatial relations, yielding poor scene-level utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 5 pedestrians and no vehicles, with a second pedestrian entry empty, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted some pedestrian attributes for one person but omitted many attributes across other pedestrians and all vehicle properties.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations field is empty which matches the ground truth's lack of explicit relation annotations, but positional/context information is provided for only one pedestrian so relations are incomplete.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures one pedestrian with some attributes but misses most people and all vehicles, yielding poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of six pedestrians and one of five vehicles, so largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Correctly labels walking and age for a pedestrian but mislabels transporting and provides limited and sometimes incorrect vehicle motion information, yielding limited attribute coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations fields are empty despite positional hints and multiple objects in the scene, so spatial relation annotations are missing.
justifications.overall: Extraction is largely incomplete with missing spatial relations and several attribute errors, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian and two vehicle entries while ground truth contains four pedestrians and six vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some per-agent attributes like action, context, and age for a pedestrian but misses communicative detail, mislabels or omits many vehicle attributes, and includes malformed keys, reducing semantic completeness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives a single coarse spatial relation for one pedestrian and leaves vehicles without spatial relations, failing to capture most relative positions.
justifications.overall: Low coverage and several semantic and formatting errors produce a poor overall extraction compared to ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses three of four pedestrians and most vehicles from the ground truth, including many object IDs and motion statuses.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides some attribute labels for one pedestrian and one vehicle but lacks many contextual and communicative details and contains malformed or empty vehicle entries.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague spatial relation is provided while other actors have no spatial relations and important distinctions like jaywalking or on road versus sidewalk are not captured.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and partially incorrect, recovering only a few basic attributes for one pedestrian and one vehicle while missing most objects and important relations.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian was reported and no vehicles, while ground truth contains seven people and four vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several attribute fields for the detected pedestrian but omits most persons, vehicle entries, and many communicative or context details present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite multiple ground truth items having specific location contexts such as on road, along the side of the road, and jaywalking.
justifications.overall: The output is largely incomplete and missing key entities and spatial semantics, only capturing a single adult pedestrian with minimal detail.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 10 annotated objects (1/10), missing all vehicles and most pedestrians.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided several attributes for the detected pedestrian (action, context, age) but missed communicative cues and attributes for other entities.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite multiple nearby pedestrians and vehicles in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Low overall performance driven by very low object recall and absent spatial information despite reasonable attribute labeling for the single detected pedestrian.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of four pedestrians and no vehicles, so largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes for the single detected pedestrian but misses many entities and contextual labels like jaywalking for other pedestrians.
justifications.spatial_relations: Leaves spatial_relations empty despite relevant road-side and crossing contexts, so spatial information is missing.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures one correct pedestrian with some attributes but misses most actors and vehicles, limiting its usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 4 pedestrians and no vehicles, capturing only about 14% of annotated entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided detailed attributes for the single detected person (actions, context, age) but failed to annotate the other pedestrians and all vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relation for the detected pedestrian is reasonable but most pedestrians and all vehicles lack spatial relations, reducing coverage.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is detailed for one correct pedestrian but severely incomplete across entities, resulting in a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 3 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing two pedestrians and all vehicle detections and attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several correct attribute labels (atomic action, context, communicative, transporting, age) for the detected person but misses complex context labels like jaywalking for the other pedestrians and omits vehicle semantics.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations are provided despite ground truth indicating on-road and jaywalking contexts, so relational location information is essentially absent.
justifications.overall: Reasonable attribute labeling for a single detected pedestrian but overall low coverage and missing vehicles lead to poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only a generic scene context and omitted all six annotated pedestrians and three vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides only a high-level urban description with no entity attributes (actions, ages, motion statuses) from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relationships or positional context for pedestrians or vehicles (eg, walking on road, alongside, parked) were reported.
justifications.overall: Fails to identify critical objects and their attributes or relations, rendering the extraction of minimal utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected no pedestrians or vehicles despite multiple annotated objects, so completeness is 0.0.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided no attribute, action, or motion information for any object, yielding zero semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial or contextual relations reported between road users or scene elements, so score is 0.0.
justifications.overall: Extraction fails to capture any annotated entities or semantics and is effectively empty.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of five pedestrians and one of three vehicles, missing the majority of ground-truth objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields (action, age, door/trunk state) for detected objects but omits object identifiers, has a misnamed vehicle key, and covers very few instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides only one pedestrian relation and no vehicle relations, lacking comprehensive spatial relations across objects.
justifications.overall: Low overall due to many missed detections despite reasonable attribute detail for the few detected objects.

justifications.completeness: Detected one out of five pedestrians and no vehicles, missing most annotated actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected pedestrian many semantic attributes were provided (action, context, age, communicative, transporting), but the extraction missed labels for other actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided a relative location for the lone detected pedestrian but omitted spatial relations for the other four pedestrians and all vehicles.
justifications.overall: Extraction gives detailed semantics for one person but is incomplete overall due to missing four pedestrians and all vehicles, resulting in moderate-low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Only one of four pedestrians was reported and both vehicles were omitted, so most relevant entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected pedestrian several semantic attributes are provided and mostly correct, but the extraction lacks additional persons and any vehicle semantics.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations field is empty and while simple_context gives some location info, explicit spatial relationships between agents and scene elements are missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction accurately labels attributes for one pedestrian but misses most entities and spatial relations, yielding low overall coverage and utility.

justifications.completeness: Misses all four pedestrians and only reports one of two vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some vehicle attributes (motion, trunk/doors) and a track hint but misidentifies vehicle type and omits pedestrian attributes and counts.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a useful 'in the distance' relation for the vehicle but lacks spatial relations for multiple pedestrians and the other vehicle.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a single distant vehicle with some attributes but misses all pedestrians and one vehicle, leading to low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Most pedestrians and one of the two annotated vehicles are missing, with only a single distant vehicle reported.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides very few object attributes, omits all pedestrian actions, counts, ages, and track ids, and uses a generic vehicle type.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes one vague relation indicating the vehicle is in the distance but omits spatial relations for multiple pedestrians and the second vehicle.
justifications.overall: While one vehicle's motion and door/trunk state are correct, the extraction misses nearly all pedestrians and many scene details, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of eight pedestrians though the vehicle was captured, resulting in major omissions in object coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: For detected objects the extraction provides several semantic attributes (actions, context, age, vehicle state), but it misses important contextual labels like jaywalking and fails to enumerate multiple pedestrians.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial information is sparse and inconsistent (empty for the pedestrian, only 'in the distance' for the vehicle) and does not capture relative positions or on-road versus sidewalk distinctions.
justifications.overall: The extraction includes useful per-object attributes but severely undercounts pedestrians and provides weak spatial detail, limiting practical usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 7 ground truth pedestrians and no vehicles, so the extraction misses the vast majority of actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected person the model provided correct atomic action, simple context, and age, but it omitted track identifiers, most other pedestrians, vehicle attributes, and special contexts like jaywalking.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations were left empty despite visual cues (on-road, near a cone) and multiple inter-object relations present in the scene.
justifications.overall: The extraction accurately describes one pedestrian at a basic level but fails to detect most actors and vehicle information, resulting in poor overall coverage and utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction notes a general scene context but fails to report any of the two pedestrians or the five vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides only a brief scene description and lacks attributes, actions, and status information for the persons and vehicles in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The description correctly mentions parked cars on the left and a construction area on the right, but it omits spatial relations for individual objects and relative positions among objects.
justifications.overall: Despite a correct coarse left versus right note, the extraction is largely incomplete and semantically sparse due to missing detections and attribute details.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses all annotated persons and vehicles, only providing a high-level scene context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides minimal semantic detail and omits all per-object attributes like actions, motion status, or door/trunk states present in ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Extraction gives coarse left/right spatial context for parked cars and construction but lacks detailed spatial relationships for each annotated object.
justifications.overall: Given the near-total omission of annotated pedestrians and vehicles and scarce semantic detail, the extraction is largely insufficient despite a correct high-level scene description.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and one of four vehicles, with one vehicle entry left blank, so most ground-truth objects and attributes are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes such as age and a vehicle motion status are provided, but important semantics are incorrect or omitted (missing biking/sitting person, no jaywalking context, many blank fields).
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vehicle left-side relation was reported and no pedestrian spatial relation or relations for the other vehicles were provided, so spatial information is largely missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains a few correct elements but misses multiple objects, has incorrect or incomplete contextual labels, and provides sparse spatial relations, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only describes the general scene and omits all annotated pedestrians and vehicles, missing nearly all required entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: There is minimal descriptive content about the scene (narrow street, buildings, power lines) but no semantic attributes for persons or vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The output mentions buildings on both sides which is a coarse spatial cue, but provides no object-level locations or relations among pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction gives a small amount of scene context but fails to detect or describe the critical dynamic actors and their properties, resulting in poor utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: The model detected the bicyclist but missed the walking person and both vehicle instances from the ground truth, so overall coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes motion and spatial relation for the bicycle but misclassifies a person as a vehicle and omits actions, ages, and other vehicle attributes, providing limited semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: The bicycle is correctly localized to the left side of the road, but no spatial relations are provided for the other annotated objects, so spatial accuracy is only partial.
justifications.overall: The output captures one object with some correct attributes but contains a major class error and misses multiple ground-truth entities, resulting in poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one vehicle and missed all pedestrians and most vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided several vehicle attributes (motion, trunk, doors, spatial hint) but misclassified a van as a car and omitted many entity types and actions.
justifications.spatial_relations: Contains a single vague relation indicating distance for the vehicle which is somewhat correct but lacks detailed spatial relations and relations for other objects.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures one object with some attributes but is largely incomplete and lacks key entities and spatial detail.

justifications.completeness: The extraction missed both pedestrians and three of four annotated vehicles, only capturing a single distant vehicle, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some object attributes were provided (type, motion status, trunk/doors, a spatial hint) but key entities and actions (pedestrians, detailed vehicle IDs) were omitted and the vehicle type lacked specificity relative to the description.
justifications.spatial_relations: A coarse spatial relation for the reported vehicle ('in the distance') is present and reasonable, but no spatial relations were provided for other objects.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor due to missing pedestrians and most vehicles, limited attribute accuracy, and sparse spatial coverage.

justifications.completeness: Only one of six annotated agents (four vehicles and two persons) was detected, so pedestrians and most vehicles are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several attributes for the detected vehicle (type, motion, doors/trunk, distance) but misses all person semantics and includes a type inconsistency and incomplete attribute grounding.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single vague relation 'in the distance' is reported while more detailed spatial relations relative to the road and other objects are absent.
justifications.overall: The output captures one distant vehicle with some attributes but fails to detect pedestrians and most vehicles, resulting in poor overall coverage and limited accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Misses the single pedestrian and most vehicles from the ground truth, reporting only one vehicle and omitting several entities and their statuses.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some vehicle attributes such as motion and door/trunk status but lacks pedestrian actions, age, multiple vehicle details, and mislabels the vehicle type as car instead of van.
justifications.spatial_relations: Correctly notes that the vehicle is in the distance but fails to supply spatial relations for the other vehicles and the person, limiting scene spatial coverage.
justifications.overall: Captures a small, partially correct subset of the scene but omits key entities and many important attributes, resulting in low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures high-level scene context and mentions a cyclist but fails to list the detected person or any of the five vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Only a brief scene description and a cyclist mention are provided with no actor attributes (age, action) or vehicle states (motion, doors), so semantic detail is minimal.
justifications.spatial_relations: The output supplies some spatial cues like buildings on both sides and a cyclist in the lane, but it does not spatially relate entities to each other or to the omitted vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction gives useful high-level context but misses critical entity listings and attribute details, yielding a low overall quality for driving-scene perception.

justifications.completeness: VLM mentioned a woman biking but failed to populate the pedestrians list and omitted all ground-truth vehicles, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output gives a brief scene description and a bicyclist mention but lacks structured semantic attributes (person age/action, vehicle states) present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The description correctly places the bicyclist in the left lane, but no other spatial relationships or object locations (e.g., vehicles) are provided.
justifications.overall: Partial correct free-text description but missing structured detections and most semantic details leads to a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction mentions a bicyclist in the scene description but fails to populate the required structured 'pedestrians' and 'vehicles' lists or any vehicle attributes from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides only a brief natural-language note of a woman riding a bicycle and no detailed semantic labels (age, actions, vehicle motion states) present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: It correctly indicates the bicyclist is in the 'left lane', but offers no other spatial relations for vehicles or scene elements.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction gives a partial descriptive mention of the bicyclist but omits required structured detections and most semantic details, resulting in low utility for downstream perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one vehicle and omits the detected person and multiple other vehicles present in the ground truth, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: It provides some useful attributes (type, motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial relation) for the single reported vehicle but lacks pedestrian attributes and information for the many other vehicles, limiting semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial relation for the reported van ('on the left side of the road') is appropriate, but only one spatial relation is provided and others are missing.
justifications.overall: Accurate for a single parked vehicle detail but missing key objects and many semantics from the scene, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian is reported out of seven and no vehicles are detected, so most annotated entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction supplies some descriptive attributes but omits many ground-truth actions, contexts, and object-specific details present in the annotations.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reported spatial relations are not supported by the provided ground truth and many plausible relations are omitted, so the spatial information is largely unverified or incorrect.
justifications.overall: Low entity coverage combined with partially informative but incomplete semantics yields an overall poor match to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian is partially described, a second entry is empty, and all annotated vehicles and most ground truth persons are missing, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several attribute fields (age, transporting, communicative) but contains incorrect or unverifiable claims (pedestrian crossing at a crossing, mention of a traffic officer) and omits many ground truth attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: A couple of spatial relations are listed for one person but they are not supported by the provided ground truth and are thus unverifiable or likely incorrect.
justifications.overall: The output shows some attribute-level detail but is incomplete, contains hallucinations, and misses most objects, resulting in poor overall utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures a few scene items but omits all annotated pedestrians and vehicles from the ground truth, resulting in very low coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: Although some scene elements (cones, crosswalk, person in vest) are described, the output lacks actions, object attributes, and vehicle states present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides coarse spatial cues (cones on the left, person on the right, crosswalk) but misses relational detail among multiple pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: A few accurate high-level descriptors are present but the failure to detect and label the many pedestrians and vehicles severely limits the extraction's usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Extraction failed to report any of the multiple pedestrians or vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Only a minimal scene-level description is provided with no object-level attributes such as actions, transport states, or motion statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides limited scene layout (narrow street, buildings both sides, trees on the left) but omits spatial relations for pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction offers a brief contextual description but misses nearly all required object detections and semantic details from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all annotated persons and vehicles despite the ground truth listing eight people and four vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Only a generic scene description and mention of cones are provided, with none of the detailed agent attributes, actions, or object states present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction notes cones on the left and that the street is narrow but gives no spatial placement for the pedestrians or vehicles that are present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Major omissions of people and vehicles result in a very low overall score despite a brief scene-level description.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses all annotated persons and vehicles, only noting a generic road and crossing and therefore captures very little of the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The description adds a specific claim about a man directing traffic but omits detailed semantic attributes like multiple persons, actions, ages, and vehicle motion states present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial relationships between objects (pedestrians and vehicles) are provided, and object-level locations relative to the scene are absent.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction provides a brief scene caption but fails to detect or describe the many annotated persons and vehicles, yielding very low utility for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Extraction failed to detect any of the seven pedestrians and four vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a basic static scene description (buildings, trees, traffic cone) but omits actors, actions, and object states found in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes coarse spatial cues (buildings on both sides, trees on the left, cone in the distance) yet lacks spatial relations involving pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Captures limited static context but misses critical dynamic entities and semantics necessary for reliable driving-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Very incomplete: only one of eight pedestrians was detected and no vehicles were reported, missing the vast majority of annotated entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several attribute fields for the single detected person (action, context, age), but most actors lack any annotations and vehicle semantics are entirely absent, limiting overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations entries were populated and many positional distinctions in the ground truth (on-road, along side, jaywalking) are not represented despite one simple_context mentioning along the side of the road.
justifications.overall: Poor overall performance: the schema and attributes are reasonable for a single detection but coverage and spatial/vehicle information are severely lacking, reducing utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one pedestrian with attributes and omits the majority of annotated people and all vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some basic attributes (walking, age, simple context) are provided for one person, but many actions (pushing, carrying, sitting/biking), contextual nuances (jaywalking), and vehicle information are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations are reported despite opportunities to describe relative positions and crossing context, and the provided arrays are empty.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides a small correct subset of attributes for a single pedestrian but misses most entities and relations, yielding poor overall coverage and usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Missed all annotated pedestrians and vehicles, only provided a generic scene description and a distant traffic cone.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides minimal semantic attributes and no actions, ages, or vehicle states from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some scene-level relations (buildings on both sides, trees to the left, cone in distance) but fails to locate or relate pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Extraction largely omits critical dynamic agents and detailed semantics, yielding low usefulness for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies one pedestrian but misses four other annotated persons and all four vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected person several attributes are provided, but many atomic actions, transporting states and contexts present in the ground truth are omitted across the scene.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction gives a plausible left sidewalk relation for the detected pedestrian but provides no spatial relations for the other people or any vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially correct for one actor but is sparse and fails to capture most actors and vehicle information, limiting its usefulness for scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Only one of five annotated people and none of the four vehicles were reported, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies basic attributes for a single person (action, context, age) but omits multiple actors, transporting details present in the ground truth, and all vehicle attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are given for the single detected pedestrian and align with sidewalk context but miss relations for other people and for all vehicles and include an unverified detail (traffic cones).
justifications.overall: Significant omissions of multiple pedestrians and all vehicles combined with limited attribute coverage produce a low overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all four annotated persons and all three vehicles, providing only a minimal scene-level description and thus covering very little of the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: While it notes a pedestrian crossing and a manhole, it contains no entity detections, actions, ages, or vehicle states that are present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The description gives a generic center location for the manhole and calls the street narrow but fails to describe spatial relations among pedestrians, vehicles, and the crossing.
justifications.overall: Because the extraction misses all annotated agents and key attributes despite a small amount of scene detail, it is largely insufficient for driving perception evaluation.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports no pedestrians or vehicles despite four annotated people and three vehicles, so it completely misses the annotated objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output gives a minimal scene caption and a likely hallucinated detail (man directing traffic) while omitting all annotated attributes like actions, transport states, ages, and vehicle statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial relationships or relative positions between entities are provided and the scene context does not convey object-to-object relations.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is largely incomplete and uninformative, missing all annotated detections and most semantic details while including at least one unsupported claim.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all five annotated pedestrians and three vehicles, capturing only a minimal scene context so it is nearly empty relative to the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides only a simple location and a brief scene note and lacks any object-level semantic attributes present in the ground truth such as actions, ages, motion status, or interactions.
justifications.spatial_relations: Although the extraction mentions a crosswalk and a foreground manhole, it fails to describe spatial relations between the many annotated people and vehicles in the scene.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is extremely sparse and misses nearly all annotated entities and their semantics, providing only a tiny fraction of the required information.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures the scene setting but completely omits all seven annotated people and the parked vehicle, missing the majority of required entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides only a brief scene description and no attributes such as actions, transport states, ages, or object statuses present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The model correctly notes trees on the left and cones blocking part of the road, but it fails to describe spatial relations of pedestrians to the road or vehicle placement relative to people.
justifications.overall: While the scene context is partly correct, the extraction largely fails to detect and describe the critical dynamic agents and their attributes, yielding poor overall coverage.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only reports one pedestrian and no vehicles while the ground truth contains six persons and four vehicles, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes like age and an action label are provided, but many atomic actions, contexts (e.g., biking, pushing), and vehicle states are omitted or inaccurately described.
justifications.spatial_relations: The claimed relation of being directed by a traffic officer is not supported in the ground truth and other spatial relations among multiple people and vehicles are not captured.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains a few plausible attributes but is largely incomplete and includes at least one incorrect relational claim, resulting in low overall fidelity to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Extremely low coverage since the model detected only one of about ten pedestrians and none of the five vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction lists multiple attributes for the single detected person but many entity-level semantics are missing and some provided attributes conflict with the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single coarse spatial relation is reported for one pedestrian which may be reasonable, but spatial relations for most pedestrians and all vehicles are absent.
justifications.overall: Overall the output shows some correct atomic action and contextual description for one person but fails to detect the majority of entities and misses or mislabels several attributes.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captured only one partially described pedestrian (and an empty placeholder) while missing the majority of annotated people and all vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains multiple attribute types (action, context, transporting, age) for one subject but many attributes are incorrect or unsupported by the ground truth and most entities are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single vague relation ('on the left side of the road') is provided for one person, but spatial relations for other pedestrians and vehicles are absent and do not capture nuanced contexts like jaywalking.
justifications.overall: The extraction is sparse and partly inaccurate, giving a few attribute labels for one person but failing to detect and describe most ground truth entities and vehicle information.

justifications.completeness: Misses all annotated persons and vehicles, providing no detections for either category.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a basic scene description (buildings, trees, manhole) but omits all semantic attributes and actions of people and vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some scene-level spatial cues such as trees on the left and a manhole in the middle but gives no spatial relations involving the annotated people or vehicles.
justifications.overall: Extraction fails to detect any annotated agents and lacks actionable semantic and spatial detail despite minor scene-level descriptions.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports no pedestrians or vehicles despite seven people and three vehicles annotated in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The scene description includes a few static details like a white line, buildings, trees, and a manhole but omits all entity-level semantics and attributes from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides basic left/right relations and an edge marking, but it lacks spatial relations involving the many pedestrians and vehicles present in the frame.
justifications.overall: Although the static scene context is somewhat described, the complete omission of annotated people and vehicles results in a very low overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: Very low since the extraction failed to detect any of the six pedestrians and six vehicles present in the ground truth, providing only a generic scene description.
justifications.semantic_richness: Poor because it offered only a brief scene-level description and omitted detailed attributes such as actions, ages, and vehicle motion states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Partial — the model captured coarse layout cues (buildings on the right, trees on the left, cones blocking the road) but missed spatial relations involving pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall reflects that while some high-level context was noted, the extraction is largely incomplete and semantically sparse, missing most annotated entities and attributes.

justifications.completeness: Detected one parked car but missed all seven pedestrians and most other vehicles from the ground truth, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes basic vehicle attributes but omits pedestrians, actions, ages, and multiple vehicles, providing limited semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single left-side relation for one car is reported while spatial contexts for pedestrians and other vehicles are absent.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and incomplete, capturing a single parked car relation but failing to report pedestrians and most vehicles.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all annotated persons and vehicles from the ground truth, reporting none of the five people or two vehicles present in the frame.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides a simple scene description but contains no semantic labels for actions, ages, motion status, or object counts present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The description captures some scene-level spatial cues like cones on the left and buildings on both sides but fails to report spatial relations for any pedestrians or vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction offers a brief scene summary but is largely incomplete and lacks the detailed semantic and object-level information required by the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses all pedestrians and most vehicles from the ground truth, reporting only a single distant vehicle and some scene context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a few attributes for one vehicle and some scene elements but omits detailed person attributes and many vehicle entries and mislabels the van as a generic car.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes basic spatial cues (right/left and visible in the distance) but lacks detailed relative positioning and relationships among the multiple objects present.
justifications.overall: Sparsely informative and partially correct for a single distant vehicle, but largely incomplete and missing the majority of ground truth annotations.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one pedestrian and omits six other annotated persons and all vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected person the model provides multiple attributes, but it fails to report most entities and vehicle properties present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single coarse spatial relation is given for one person but spatial relations for other people and vehicles are missing or not detailed.
justifications.overall: The extraction shows some per-entity detail for one pedestrian but misses the majority of entities and scene elements, yielding a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detected one vehicle reasonably but missed all seven pedestrians and left a second vehicle entry blank, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some vehicle attributes (type, motion status, trunk and doors) were provided for one car, but many fields are empty and no pedestrian semantics were reported.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial relation for the left-side car was given, but spatial context for the other vehicle and all pedestrians is missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides a few correct vehicle attributes but is largely incomplete and omits all pedestrians, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of seven pedestrians and omitted the parked vehicle, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected person the model provides useful attributes (action, context, age) but it misses many actors and some transport/communicative details present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extracted spatial relation for the detected person is reasonable, but spatial information is missing for most pedestrians and the vehicle.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor because most entities are missed despite accurate labeling for the one detected pedestrian.

justifications.completeness: The extraction includes only one of five ground-truth pedestrians while correctly reporting the vehicle, so it misses the majority of annotated persons and is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The model provides useful attributes for the reported pedestrian and vehicle (action, context, age, motion and door/trunk status) but omits most other pedestrians and some object-level details, yielding moderate semantic richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: The vehicle spatial relation on the left side of the road is correctly reported, but pedestrian spatial relations are left empty despite ground-truth side-of-road contexts, so spatial coverage is partial.
justifications.overall: The extraction correctly identifies and labels one pedestrian and the parked vehicle with several attributes but misses most pedestrians and their spatial relations, resulting in a low-to-moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction omits all three pedestrians and the parked vehicle, only providing a generic scene description.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction lacks detailed attributes such as actions, ages, and vehicle motion status present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a coarse location 'road' is reported and no relations like walking along the side or parked near curb are captured.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures general scene context but fails to detect and describe the people and vehicle required for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of five pedestrians and omitted the parked vehicle, missing most annotated entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes (action, age, simple context) and an appearance hint for a single person but omits many annotated attributes across other persons (including transporting and varying ages) and other actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations entries provided despite ground truth containing positional contexts (on road vs along side), so relational information is effectively missing.
justifications.overall: Low overall quality because the extraction captures one person reasonably but fails to report most actors, vehicle presence, and relational details from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 7 pedestrians and no vehicles, so the majority of annotated actors are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels (action, simple context, age) for a single person but omits multiple actors and important attributes like transporting for another person and age variation (senior).
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations field is empty despite multiple actors in the scene and contextual location cues.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is low because most pedestrians and the vehicle are missing, even though the extraction gives correct basic labels for one person.
